headline: Rossi out at Columbus as three new faces arrive
dek: Diego Rossi departs the Crew while Austin FC, Columbus, and D.C. United each add a body.
body: Diego Rossi has left Columbus Crew after logging 1,290 minutes this season. His destination is unknown. Columbus moved quickly to offset the loss, bringing in Lautaro Giaccone. Austin FC have added Alonso Ramírez, while D.C. United have signed Fidel Barajas. The Crew's business is the most pointed of the three, coming on the same day their departed forward's season total was confirmed.

headline: Cuypers holds one-goal cushion in tight scoring race
dek: Chicago's Hugo Cuypers leads on 13 goals, with Petar Musa and Leo Messi one back at 12 apiece.
body: Hugo Cuypers of Chicago Fire FC sits atop the golden boot standings with 13 goals. FC Dallas striker Petar Musa and Inter Miami's Leo Messi are level in second at 12. Messi adds seven assists to his ledger, putting him in the thick of the creative race as well. Son Heung-Min of LAFC leads that category outright with eight, one clear of Messi and San Diego FC's Anders Dreyer.

headline: Ingvartsen and Guilherme beating the model by wide margins
dek: San Diego's Marcus Ingvartsen is outperforming his xG by 4.3 goals; Houston's Guilherme trails only slightly at plus-4.2.
body: Marcus Ingvartsen has scored 11 goals against an xG of 6.8, a difference of plus-4.3, the largest overperformance in the league. Guilherme of Houston Dynamo FC is close behind at plus-4.2, with eight goals from 3.8 expected. At the other end, Vancouver's Brian White carries the league's largest deficit, sitting 2.4 goals below his xG of 12.4 despite already reaching double figures with ten.

headline: Nashville and San Jose lead their conferences by a point
dek: Nashville SC hold the East on 33 points; San Jose Earthquakes top the West on 32 with the sharper attack.
body: Nashville SC lead the Eastern Conference with 33 points from a 10-3-1 record and the stingiest defense in the league, having conceded just 11 goals. San Jose Earthquakes front the West on 32 points at 10-2-3, posting 34 goals scored. Inter Miami CF own the best attack in MLS with 39 goals for the season, though their defensive numbers have kept them off the top line in the East.

headline: Tiago, Palacio swap addresses in MLS
dek: Two midweek deals shift pieces between New England, Orlando, Salt Lake, and Toronto.
body: Tiago moves from New England Revolution to Orlando City SC, adding depth to a side that has been active in the market. Nelson Palacio heads in the opposite direction of the league map, leaving Real Salt Lake for Toronto FC. Neither club released contract details. The moves leave the Revolution and RSL with roster holes to fill before the next selection window closes.

headline: Cuypers holds a one-goal edge at the top
dek: Chicago's Belgian striker leads Musa and Messi by a single goal with the season still live.
body: Hugo Cuypers of Chicago Fire FC sits at 13 goals, one clear of FC Dallas forward Petar Musa and Inter Miami CF's Leo Messi, who are level at 12. Messi is also second in the assists table with seven, behind LAFC's Son Heung-Min, who leads that category at eight. Anders Dreyer of San Diego FC shares second in assists. Three players within one goal of the boot is as tight a race as the league has produced this term.

headline: Ingvartsen and Guilherme defy the model
dek: Two forwards are each outscoring their expected-goals marks by more than four goals.
body: Marcus Ingvartsen of San Diego FC has 11 goals against an xG of 6.8, a positive gap of plus-4.3. Houston Dynamo FC's Guilherme is close behind at plus-4.2, with eight goals from an xG of 3.8. On the other side of the ledger, Vancouver's Brian White holds 10 goals but carries an xG of 12.4, leaving him minus-2.4. Minnesota's Kelvin Yeboah is minus-1.8, with eight goals against an xG of 9.8.

headline: Nashville and San Jose lead their tables
dek: A combined two points separate the Eastern and Western leaders as the season reaches full stride.
body: Nashville SC top the East with 33 points from a 10-3-1 record. Their 11 goals conceded is the best defensive mark in the league. San Jose Earthquakes lead the West on 32 points, record 10-2-3, and have scored 34 times, second only to Inter Miami CF, whose 39 goals for is the highest total in MLS. Nashville's attack and defense combination makes them the most complete side on paper through this stage.

headline: Cuypers holds one-goal edge at the top
dek: The Chicago striker leads at 13, but Musa and Messi are a single finish behind with plenty of season remaining.
body: Hugo Cuypers sits at 13 goals for Chicago Fire FC, one clear of Petar Musa (FC Dallas) and Leo Messi (Inter Miami CF), who are level on 12. Messi complicates the picture further by ranking second in assists with seven, making him the race's most versatile threat. Cuypers holds the advantage for now, but the margin is thin enough that a quiet week from Chicago could hand the lead to either challenger.

headline: Son leads assists; Messi and Dreyer give chase
dek: LAFC's Son Heung-Min sits on eight assists, one ahead of a pair of players separated by nothing at all.
body: Son Heung-Min has distributed eight assists for LAFC, the most in the league at this stage. Behind him, Leo Messi of Inter Miami CF and Anders Dreyer of San Diego FC are deadlocked on seven. San Diego's expansion-season production through Dreyer is a notable data point. Son needs only to maintain his current rate to hold the lead, but the gap is narrow enough that one decisive performance changes the order.

headline: Ingvartsen and White sit at opposite extremes
dek: San Diego's Ingvartsen is outscoring his xG by 4.3 goals; Vancouver's White is leaving 2.4 on the table.
body: Marcus Ingvartsen leads all overperformers with 11 goals against an xG of 6.8, a positive difference of plus-4.3. Houston's Guilherme is close behind at plus-4.2 on just 3.8 expected goals. At the other end, Brian White of Vancouver has scored 10 but carries an xG of 12.4, a gap of minus-2.4. Minnesota's Kelvin Yeboah trails his model by minus-1.8. Both underperformers remain productive in volume terms, which softens the concern.

headline: Nashville defense sets the standard; San Jose leads West
dek: Nashville's 11 goals allowed is the best defensive record in MLS, while Inter Miami's attack has produced 39 goals, most in the league.
body: Nashville SC tops the East on 33 points with a 10-3-1 record and have conceded only 11 goals across 14 matches, the stingiest mark in the league. San Jose Earthquakes lead the West on 32 points at 10-2-3 and have scored 34 times. Inter Miami CF own the most potent attack in MLS with 39 goals for, though their defensive numbers do not appear in the top marks. The two conference leaders are separated by a single point.
